old_claim.note: Rifling in Firearms provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from rifling_firearms to interchangeable_parts because rifles were an important application context, while rifling itself was not the source-backed manufacturing prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: NPS says Hall perfected precision machinery for producing rifles with interchangeable parts and identifies drilling, straight-cutting, and other special machines.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports armory precision machinery as the enabling mechanism for interchangeable rifle parts.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Rifling describes helical barrel grooves, while the cited interchangeable-parts mechanism is about machine tools and uniform component fabrication.

ontology_before: Interchangeable Parts depended on Rifling in Firearms, making a firearms feature appear to cause a broad manufacturing method.
ontology_after: Interchangeable Parts depends on precision machine tools; firearms remain historical application context without a direct rifling prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the rifle-feature edge was removed from the broader manufacturing node.
would_reject_if: The interchangeable_parts node were narrowed specifically to interchangeable rifled barrels.
A source showed rifling grooves were required before interchangeable locks, stocks, or other standardized parts could be manufactured.
The rifling_firearms node were expanded to represent armory manufacturing practice rather than barrel-groove machining.
short_reason: Rifling is an application-domain feature, not the manufacturing substrate.
